calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Issuance Of Peremptory Writ And Preliminary And Permanent Injunction Commanding Respondent Department To Cancel Contract With Qt Medical, Inc
Off Calendar Mar-10-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
George W. Wolff, Esq. (415.788.1881; george@wolfflaw.com) for Petitioner, Compumed, Inc.
Jennifer Dauer, Esq. (916.492.5000; jdauer@diepenbrock.com) of Diepenbrock Elkin Dauer McCandless LLP for Petitioner, Compumed, Inc.
Sarah Fabian, Esq. (415.510.3517; sarah.fabian@doj.ca.gov) Deputy Attorney General for Respondents, California Department of Corrections and Rehabilitation/California Correctional Health Care Services.
Josh Deitz, Esq. (415.956.2828; jdeitz@rjo.com) and Dennis Callahan, Esq. (dcallahan@rjo.com) of Rogers Joseph O'Donnell for Real Party in Interest, QT Medical, Inc.

A R G U E D. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PETITIONER COMPUMED, INC.'S Motion For Issuance Of Peremptory Writ And Preliminary And Permanent Injunction Commanding Respondent Department To Cancel Contract With Qt Medical, Inc. is taken under submission.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

*** Order DENYING PETITIONER'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE signed after hearing.***

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Full Responses Of Respondents-Defendants Department Of Corrections And Rehabilitation - California Correctional Healthcare Services To Petitioner-Plaintiff Compumed Inc Doing Business As Delaware Compumed Incs Request For Production Of Documents Set One
Off Calendar Dec-03-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Atty. GEORGE WILLIAM WOLFF, Esq. of Law Office of George W. Wolff (Tel: 415-788-1881) for petitioner/plaintiff CompuMed, Inc.
Atty. SARAH L. FABIAN, Esq. of California Attorney General's Office (Tel: 415-510-3517) for respondent/defendant California Department of Corrections and Rehabilitation - California Correctional Health Care Service;
Atty. AARON P. SILBERMAN, Esq. of Rogers Joseph O'Donnell (Tel: 415-956-2828) for Real Party in Interest QT Medical, Inc. (via Zoom videoconference).

HEARING RE: PETITIONER COMPUMED, INC.'s Motion To Compel Full Responses Of Respondents-Defendants Department Of Corrections And Rehabilitation - California Correctional Healthcare Services To Petitioner-Plaintiff Compumed Inc Doing Business As Delaware Compumed Incs Request For Production Of Documents Set One.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Compumed, Inc.'s three discovery motions are denied. The judge pro tem's report and recommendation is adopted. Discovery generally does not occur in CCP 1085 proceedings. If Compumed believes the record is deficient, it may say so in its writ papers.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)
